Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated creatinine, suggesting impaired renal function, and mildly decreased total CO₂, possibly indicating mild metabolic acidosis. Liver enzymes (ALT) are slightly elevated, and lipid profile reveals high LDL, high triglycerides, and low HDL, indicating increased cardiovascular risk. Other parameters are within normal limits. Clinical correlation and further evaluation of renal and hepatic function, as well as cardiovascular risk management, are recommended.
